content:
- Read current task and executed plan (NOW EXECUTE) for splitting ultra-long HA no-quorum e2e into shorter parallel-safe tests.
- Verified gates; initially hit `make test` 120s timeout and a flaky no-quorum HA-state poll (response missing status line).
- Implemented: held port reservations with JIT release; shortened/robust HA polling for no-quorum; reduced workload SQL timeouts; improved postgres shutdown fallback by killing postmaster PID when pg_ctl times out; refactored no-quorum fencing test to use quorum-loss cutoff (keeps runtime under 120s).
- Added mapping doc under docs and updated SUMMARY; Makefile enforces 120s `make test` timeout and rejects RUST_TEST_THREADS=1; `make test-long` has no timeout and currently no ultra-long tests.
- Final verification: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test` (cargo test ~93.82s under 120s wrapper), `make test-long` all green.
- Marked task md completed/passing, ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, committed + pushed.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and checked for any bug tasks failing (<passes>false</passes> / <passing>false</passing>) and for meta-tasks.
- All bug tasks are currently marked passing (<passes>true</passes> <passing>true</passing>).
- Found several non-bug tasks marked <passes>false</passes> (docs verification, structured logging, secure explicit node config story).
- Chose next task: .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/38-task-unified-structured-logging-and-postgres-binary-ingestion.md (unblocked, enables better debugging/ops signal for upcoming larger refactors).
- Wrote that path to .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Investigated postgres process management and logging paths in pgtuskmaster runtime + test harness.
- Confirmed runtime starts/stops postgres via process worker building `pg_ctl` commands; command runner sets stdin/stdout/stderr to null, so no subprocess stderr/stdout capture in production.
- Confirmed startup path in runtime/node uses same build_command + TokioCommandRunner sync loop with timeout/cancel; also no stdout/stderr capture.
- Confirmed `StartPostgresSpec` always includes `-l <postgres.log_file>` and `-o "-h ... -p ... -k ..."`; no runtime code sets postgresql.conf logging_collector/jsonlog/archive_command/log rotation.
- Found only existing tailing logic is test utilities (`read_log_tail`) and process worker integration test fallback that reads last lines of postgres.log on startup failure.
- Found harness direct postgres runner (`test_harness/pg16.rs`) separately captures process stdout/stderr into files for test-only process spawn path.
- Observed cleanup behavior: test namespaces removed on drop; logs ephemeral unless artifact_root copied; no rotation/cleanup in production.
- Need to propose phased plan for unified structured log stream: source adapters (pg_ctl -l file + postgresql jsonlog/csv/plain + archive command + startup stderr), robust file tailer with inode+offset+truncate/rename handling, normalization schema, and sink into internal state/debug API.

- Investigated repo-wide logging setup and runtime bootstrap for user request.
- Confirmed no logging facade/subscriber crates are present in Cargo.toml (no tracing/log/env_logger/etc.).
- Found current behavior: only terminal prints in bin entrypoints (`pgtuskmaster` and `pgtuskmasterctl`), Postgres child stdout/stderr suppressed in process worker, and pg_ctl writes PostgreSQL logs to configured `postgres.log_file`.
- Mapped config surfaces: `postgres.log_file` is the only log-related runtime field; no app log level/format/sink config exists.
- Mapped runtime boot path: `run_node_from_config_path -> run_node_from_config -> execute_startup -> run_workers`; there is no app logger init in runtime or workers.
- Noted observability docs mention "node logs" but implementation currently relies mostly on debug snapshot timeline/state API, not structured process logs.
- Next: provide concise report with file references and recommendations for single JSONL stderr entrypoint including required fields severity/hostname/ts_ms/source attribution.

- Investigated helper-binary invocation paths and stdout/stderr handling across process worker, runtime startup, pg16 harness, and HA e2e utilities.
- Confirmed `TokioCommandRunner` currently spawns process jobs with stdin/stdout/stderr all set to null, so helper output is discarded in production path; only pg_ctl `-l <postgres.log>` captures postgres server logs.
- Mapped real-postgres integration/e2e coverage currently in `src/process/worker.rs` tests, `src/pginfo/worker.rs` tests, `src/test_harness/pg16.rs`, and HA harness modules; these validate binary execution and some log-tail-on-failure behavior but not unified ingestion.
- Verified there are no in-tree `archive_command`/archive-mode references yet.
- Located existing task spec for unified structured logging ingestion in `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/38-task-unified-structured-logging-and-postgres-binary-ingestion.md` and prepared gap-based test-plan additions for parse-failure, archive_command capture, and rotation/cleanup.
- Next if continuing: implement logging collector + parser modules, add real-binary e2e around JSON/plain/stderr/archive/helper outputs, and add deterministic rotation/cleanup tests.

- Read previous progress log.
- Read .ralph/current_task.txt -> .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/38-task-unified-structured-logging-and-postgres-binary-ingestion.md.
- Read task 38 and explored repo for current logging/PG log plumbing.
- Spawned 3 explorer subagents to map: current logging bootstrap (none), postgres log sources (pg_ctl -l + harness file capture), helper-binary stdio handling (currently discarded), and existing test harness entry points.
- Wrote a detailed implementation plan into .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/38-task-unified-structured-logging-and-postgres-binary-ingestion.md and ended it with 'TO BE VERIFIED' as instructed.

- Notes/risks:
  - Repo currently has no logging crate; task will require new logging module + config schema changes + wiring into runtime + worker ctxs.
  - Runtime process worker currently sets stdout/stderr to null for helper binaries; must change to piped capture without deadlocks.
  - Postgres jsonlog/archive_command sources are not configured by runtime today; plan supports ingestion when present and adds optional archive wrapper for tests.
  - Must avoid unwrap/panic/expect everywhere (clippy restrictions are strict across lib + tests).

- Next (after quitting due to TO BE VERIFIED):
  - Re-open task file, do deep skeptical review, and alter at least one part of the plan.
  - Only after verification, replace TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE, then implement and run make check/test/test-long/lint.

- Reviewed postgres startup/logging wiring and real-binary harness paths for request on logging + e2e realism.
- Confirmed runtime/process-worker postgres startup uses pg_ctl with `-l <log_file>` and `-o "-h ... -p ... -k ..."`; helper binaries are spawned with stdout/stderr nulled in TokioCommandRunner so output is dropped.
- Confirmed no production log collector/tailer exists; only failure diagnostics use log tail helpers in test harness/process-worker tests.
- Mapped real-binary harness capabilities: direct postgres spawn in test_harness/pg16.rs captures stdout/stderr files; HA harness uses runtime path with postgres.log file per node and tails on readiness failure; etcd harness demonstrates robust stdout/stderr file redirection pattern.
- Confirmed no archive_command wiring exists in code/config today.
- Prepared review output with concrete file/line refs and plan changes focused on realistic coverage with minimal flake: split ingestion tests by transport path, isolate deterministic WAL/archive triggers, and keep parser/rotation mostly unit-level.

- Reviewed process runner + worker wiring for command execution and output capture behavior.
- Confirmed `ProcessCommandSpec` has only `{program,args}` and `TokioCommandRunner::spawn` sets stdin/stdout/stderr to null, so production process jobs discard helper output completely.
- Traced usage in both process worker path and runtime startup path (`run_startup_job`), both using `TokioCommandRunner` + `ProcessHandle::poll_exit`/`cancel` polling loop.
- Mapped process state surfaces (`JobOutcome`, `ProcessError`, debug view labels) to assess where bounded stdout/stderr snippets can be propagated with low API churn.
- Identified existing async pipe read pattern in ha_e2e util (`run_psql_statement`) that uses `tokio::spawn(read_to_end)` for stdout/stderr; works in tests but not ideal for core worker because we want to avoid spawn/join constraints in current_thread fixtures.
- Verified tokio `Child::wait_with_output` internals use concurrent wait+reads without explicit spawn but are unbounded Vec capture; recommended adapting to bounded capture loops.
- Proposed design: add capture policy to ProcessCommandSpec, pipe only when enabled, maintain in-handle bounded tail buffers for stdout/stderr, expose async `poll_exit` that opportunistically polls a pinned completion future via zero-timeout and avoids worker blocking/spawned tasks.
- Proposed bounded buffer implementation: fixed cap with drop-oldest semantics, chunked async reads, hard cap (e.g. 64KiB per stream) to prevent memory blowup and command deadlocks.
- Proposed compatibility strategy: keep default discard behavior for noisy long-lived jobs, enable capture for startup/basebackup/rewind and for failure diagnostics; include captured tails in `ProcessError::EarlyExit`/`SpawnFailure` context.
- Next: if asked to implement, change ProcessHandle trait + FakeHandle tests, add capture policy fields and parser defaults, wire runtime startup error messages to include captured stderr tail, and add deterministic tests for bounded truncation + deadlock-safe completion with high-output helper binary.

- Mapped config flow for RuntimeConfig/PartialRuntimeConfig: schema in src/config/schema.rs, defaults in src/config/defaults.rs, parse+validation in src/config/parser.rs.
- Verified runtime wiring path: pgtuskmaster bin -> run_node_from_config_path -> load_runtime_config -> run_node_from_config; run_node_from_config currently re-validates config before startup and worker spin-up.
- Located startup bootstrap paths: runtime/node execute_startup drives initial Bootstrap/BaseBackup/StartPostgres before workers; HA worker can also enqueue RunBootstrap during rewinding/bootstrapping recovery phases.
- Enumerated all RuntimeConfig struct literal constructors that will compile-break if a new required logging field is added (16 sites across src/tests/examples), plus the lone PartialRuntimeConfig literal in defaults tests.
- Noted migration risk: multi-node in-process e2e starts multiple runtimes via run_node_from_config, so any global logging bootstrap must be Once-guarded and tolerant of repeated calls.
- Next if continuing: implement logging schema structs + defaults/validation and add shared test helper builder to collapse duplicated RuntimeConfig literals before/with migration.

- Read .ralph/current_task.txt -> .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/38-task-unified-structured-logging-and-postgres-binary-ingestion.md.
- Opened task 38 and confirmed plan was in TO BE VERIFIED state.
- Did deep skeptical verification with 3 parallel explorer subagents:
  - Config/wiring: identified all RuntimeConfig/PartialRuntimeConfig constructors that will break; recommended optional PartialLoggingConfig + defaults; bootstrap best seam is runtime/node.rs run_node_from_config and must be idempotent across multi-node in-proc tests.
  - Process runner: stdout/stderr are currently Stdio::null; piping without draining deadlocks; recommended step-driven capture with async poll_exit + bounded drain, no tokio::spawn.
  - Postgres harness/tests: runtime starts postgres via pg_ctl -l log file only; no existing collector; best to split real-binary tests by transport (direct-spawn for jsonlog+stderr, runtime/pg_ctl plain log separately) and keep archive wrapper deterministic.
- Updated the task plan accordingly (changed FileTailer start position semantics; changed subprocess capture plan to no-spawn step-driven drain; split real-binary tests into transport-specific tests).
- Replaced trailing marker in task 38 from TO BE VERIFIED -> NOW EXECUTE.

- Next:
  - Follow the plan as written (no more exploration) and start implementing logging subsystem + ingestion + process output capture, then run full gates: make check/test/test-long/lint.
